from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    Handles multiple subjects and combines related facts into a single coherent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to strip surrounding quotes from objects
    def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        o = o.strip()
        if (o.startswith('"') and o.endswith('"')) or (o.startswith("'") and o.endswith("'")):
            return o[1:-1]
        return o

    # Group triples by subject
    from collections import defaultdict
    subj_map: dict[str, dict[str, str]] = defaultdict(dict)
    for t in triples:
        subj_map[t.subject][t.predicate] = clean_obj(t.object)

    sentences = []

    for subj, preds in subj_map.items():
        # Special handling for architect + location
        if "architect" in preds and "location" in preds:
            location = preds["location"]
            architect = preds["architect"]
            sentence = f"The architect of {subj} located in {location} was {architect}"
            sentences.append(sentence)
            continue

        # Special handling for ethnicGroup
        if "ethnicGroup" in preds:
            group = preds["ethnicGroup"]
            sentence = f"{group} people are an ethnic group in {subj}"
            sentences.append(sentence)
            continue

        # General handling using mapping tables
        # Invert map for predicates where object should come first
        invert_map = {
            "leader": "{obj} is the leader of {subj}",
            "country": "{subj} is in {obj}",
            "isPartOf": "{subj} is part of {obj}",
            "capital": "{obj} is the capital of {subj}",
            "language": "{obj} is spoken in {subj}",
            "currency": "{subj} uses the currency {obj}",
            "governmentType": "{subj} has a government type of {obj}",
            "governingBody": "{subj} is governed by {obj}",
            "origin": "{subj} originates from {obj}",
            "birthPlace": "{subj} was born in {obj}",
            "deathPlace": "{subj} died in {obj}",
            "foundationPlace": "{subj} was founded in {obj}",
        }

        default_map = {
            "architecturalStyle": "{subj} has an architectural style of {obj}",
            "buildingStartDate": "{subj} started construction in {obj}",
            "completionDate": "{subj} was completed in {obj}",
            "floorCount": "{subj} has {obj} floors",
            "location": "{subj} is located in {obj}",
            "cost": "{subj} costs {obj}",
            "floorArea": "{subj} covers a floor area of {obj} square metres",
            "architect": "{subj} was designed by {obj}",
            "owner": "{subj} is owned by {obj}",
            "formerName": "{subj} was formerly known as {obj}",
            "height": "{subj} has a height of {obj} metres",
            "buildingType": "{subj} is a {obj}",
            "developer": "{subj} was developed by {obj}",
            "tenant": "{subj} has a tenant {obj}",
            "currentTenants": "{subj} currently houses {obj}",
            "address": "{subj} has the address {obj}",
            "inaugurationDate": "{subj} was inaugurated on {obj}",
            "birthPlace": "{subj} was born in {obj}",
            "deathPlace": "{subj} died in {obj}",
            "bedCount": "{subj} has {obj} beds",
            "region": "{subj} is in the region of {obj}",
            "state": "{subj} is in the state of {obj}",
            "website": "{subj} has website {obj}",
            "yearOfConstruction": "{subj} was constructed in {obj}",
            "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "{subj} has NRHP reference number {obj}",
            "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "{subj} was added to the NRHP on {obj}",
            "significantBuilding": "{subj} is known for the building {obj}",
            "significantProject": "{subj} is known for the project {obj}",
            "demonym": "{subj} has demonym {obj}",
            "numberOfRooms": "{subj} has {obj} rooms",
            "keyPerson": "{subj} has key person {obj}",
            "architecture": "{subj} features architecture {obj}",
            "postalCode": "{subj} has postal code {obj}",
            "language": "{subj} speaks {obj}",
            "leaderTitle": "{subj} has leader title {obj}",
            "ethnicGroup": "{subj} has ethnic group {obj}",
            "currency": "{subj} uses currency {obj}",
        }

        # Build sentence for each predicate not already handled
        for pred, obj in preds.items():
            if pred in ("architect", "location", "ethnicGroup"):
                continue  # already handled
            if pred in invert_map:
                sentence = invert_map[pred].format(subj=subj, obj=obj)
            else:
                template = default_map.get(pred, "{subj} {obj}")
                sentence = template.format(subj=subj, obj=obj)
            sentences.append(sentence)

    # Combine sentences into a single fluent paragraph
    if not sentences:
        return ""

    if len(sentences) == 1:
        result = sentences[0]
    else:
        result = "; ".join(sentences)

    return result[0].upper() + result[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
